candidate_summary: Multi-modal 5-stage flow-drill skill-ladder (token/reasoning/plan/tool/multi-agent) with per-stage long-medium-close range-partition sub-curriculum, pace-locked mastery-threshold acceleration, and cross-modality late-early back-feed gradient.

results.name: Multimodal Reasoning Staged RL (2506.04207)
results.description: LLM-side: optimized cold start + staged RL phases multimodal reasoning; gradient stagnation handled via staged transition.
keyword_matches: multi-modal flow-drill skill-ladder stage (partial: multi-modal + staged)
results.rationale: Staged RL phases for multimodal reasoning aligns with multi-modal stage ladder. Lacks explicit range-partition, pace-lock mastery threshold, and cross-modality back-feed. Same artefact (staged curriculum) but partial effect coverage.

results.name: MAPS Advancing Multi-Modal Reasoning ICLR 2025
results.description: LLM-side: multi-modal reasoning staged training for vision + reasoning + tool integration.
keyword_matches: multi-modal flow-drill skill-ladder stage (partial: multi-modal staged)
single-stick empty-hand knife sword progression (partial: vision+reasoning+tool integration analog)
results.rationale: Multi-modal staged training across vision/reasoning/tool maps to skill-ladder progression spanning modalities. Same artefact (staged multi-modal curriculum) and similar effect of cross-stage skill transfer. Lacks pace-lock and back-feed.

results.name: Curriculum Design Trajectory-Constrained Agent CoT Compression (2511.02690)
results.description: LLM-side: curriculum gradually tightens constraints + smoother transition + CoT compression.
keyword_matches: pace-locked threshold-graduated acceleration (partial: gradual tightening transition)
results.rationale: Gradual constraint tightening is a single-axis curriculum without multi-modal ladder, range partition, or back-feed. Different artefact (constraint schedule vs modality ladder); effect overlaps only at the level of pacing.

results.name: CAMPUS Competence-Aware Multi-Perspective Curriculum Instruction (2509.13790)
results.description: LLM-side: CAMPUS competence-aware curriculum with dynamic difficulty + adversarial-learned scoring.
keyword_matches: pace-locked threshold-graduated acceleration (partial: competence-aware dynamic difficulty)
results.rationale: Competence-aware dynamic difficulty matches the pace-lock mastery threshold concept. However missing multi-modal ladder, range partition, and back-feed. Same effect (mastery-gated pacing) but only one of four mechanisms.

results.name: VL-Cogito Progressive Curriculum RL (2507.22607)
results.description: LLM-side: PCuRL three-stage easy/medium/hard with difficulty soft-weighting.
results.rationale: Three-stage progressive curriculum is structurally similar but single-modality, no range partition, no back-feed. Candidate explicitly differentiates from this paper.

results.name: Curriculum RL Easy-to-Hard LLM Reasoning
results.description: LLM-side: easy-to-hard task curriculum RL with probabilistic scheduler + foundational skill build-up.
results.rationale: Easy-to-hard probabilistic scheduler is generic curriculum without multi-modal ladder or back-feed. Different artefact.

results.name: WISDOM Progressive Curriculum Synthesis Math
results.description: LLM-side: gradually synthesize CoT data easy-to-hard.
results.rationale: CoT synthesis easy-to-hard is single-modality data generation, not multi-modal ladder. Candidate explicitly differentiates.

results.name: Pre-Training Curriculum Multi-Token Prediction LM (2505.22757)
results.description: LLM-side: pre-training curriculum for multi-token prediction in language models.
results.rationale: Multi-token prediction curriculum is token-level only; lacks multi-modal ladder, range partition, pace-lock, back-feed. Different artefact and effect.

decision_rationale: Independent scoring finds 2 hits among 8 results (Multimodal Staged RL 2506.04207 and MAPS ICLR 2025), both crossing thresholds on the multi-modal staged-curriculum dimension. Prior art covers the core combination of multi-modal + staged skill ladder, so candidate is not sufficiently novel. R464 SINAWALI-FLOW FAILS the novelty bar.
